# ✅ Supported Versions & Platforms

To ensure the best experience and most accurate detection, it is important to run **VeinGuard** on a supported platform and Minecraft version.

---

## 🏗️ Compatibility Matrix

We focus our development and testing on the most modern and performant server software.

### Legend
*   ✅ **Supported:** Fully tested and officially supported.
*   ⚠️ **Untested:** May work, but use at your own risk. No official support provided.
*   ❌ **Unsupported:** Known to be incompatible or not planned for support.

| Minecraft Version | Paper | Spigot | Purpur | Folia |
| :--- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| **1.21.x +** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| **1.20.x** | ✅ | ✅ | ⚠️ | ❌ |
| **1.19.x** | ✅ | ✅ | ⚠️ | ❌ |
| **1.18.x** | ✅ | ✅ | ⚠️ | ❌ |
| **1.17.x & Older** | ⚠️ | ⚠️ | ⚠️ | ❌ |

---

## 📋 Platform Specifics

### 🟢 Spigot & Paper
VeinGuard is primarily built and tested on **Paper** (and its upstream, **Spigot**). We recommend using Paper for the best performance and compatibility with modern Minecraft versions.

### 🟣 Purpur
Support for **Purpur** is officially confirmed for version **1.21 and newer**. While older versions of Purpur likely work (as it is a Paper fork), they have not undergone rigorous testing.

### 🔴 Folia
There are currently **no plans** to support Folia. VeinGuard's architecture, particularly its real-time event tracking and database handling, is not currently optimized for Folia's multi-threaded region system.

---

## ☕ Java Requirements
Regardless of your Minecraft version, VeinGuard requires **Java 17 or newer** to run. Most modern server hosts provide this by default, but you should verify your startup flags if you encounter "Unsupported Class Version" errors.

---

## 🛡️ Third-Party Hooks
*   **WorldGuard:** Supported on versions compatible with Minecraft 1.18+.
*   **LuckPerms:** Fully supported for permission management.

---

*If you are using a version marked as Untested (⚠️) and encounter issues, please let us know on [Discord](https://discord.gg/sW7zu4RXmD), though we may prioritize support for officially supported versions.*
